title: Introduction to ctdR
text: ctdR identifies chemicals significantly associated set genes using data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD). Four enrichment methods supported single enrichment_CTD() interface; input shape depends method: ORA (-Representation Analysis) – gene list + hypergeometric test. Powered clusterProfiler::enricher. GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) – ranked gene list + permutation test. Powered fgsea::fgsea. CAMERA (competitive gene-set test) – expression matrix + design + contrast. Corrects inter-gene correlation within chemical’s gene set. Powered limma::camera. GSVA (Gene Set Variation Analysis) – expression matrix + per-sample scoring. Returns chemical x sample score matrix. Powered GSVA::gsva.

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: package bundle, redistribute, embed data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database. CTD data created maintained NC State University subject specific licensing terms. Users must download data directly https://ctdbase.org comply CTD Terms Service.

text: ctdR workflow three steps: Download CTD data file (, manually) Import data ctdR () Analyse gene list (many times needed)

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: Download CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz https://ctdbase.org/reports/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz. decompress : produces CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv (several GB uncompressed).

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: production run: vignette use small synthetic dataset bundled package: import_CTD() performs following: Reads CSV (skipping 27 CTD header lines). Filters interactions Homo sapiens (OrganismID 9606). Collects Entrez gene IDs chemical. Maps Entrez IDs HGNC gene symbols via org.Hs.eg.db. Caches processed data locally (rappdirs::user_cache_dir("ctdR")). step takes several minutes full CTD file. need run – download newer CTD release.
code: library(ctdR) import_CTD("~/Downloads/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv") library(ctdR) sample_file <- system.file(     "extdata", "CTD_chem_gene_ixns_sample.csv",     package = "ctdR" ) import_CTD(sample_file) #> Reading CTD chemical-gene interactions from: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.6/Resources/library/ctdR/extdata/CTD_chem_gene_ixns_sample.csv #> Filtered to 86 human interactions #> Mapping genes for 10 chemicals (this may take a while)... #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> CTD data cached successfully in: ~/Library/Caches/ctdR

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: ORA tests whether overlap gene list chemical’s known gene targets significantly larger expected chance. ORA output columns:
code: ora_results <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA") #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #>  head(ora_results) #>   ChemicalID GeneRatio BgRatio RichFactor FoldEnrichment    zScore      pvalue #> 1    D000082     10/10   12/17  0.8333333       1.416667 3.0860670 0.003393665 #> 2    D001151      6/10   10/17  0.6000000       1.020000 0.1142857 0.646133278 #> 3    D001564      8/10   10/17  0.8000000       1.360000 2.0571429 0.052241876 #> 4    D002945      9/10   11/17  0.8181818       1.390909 2.5305065 0.017533937 #>         padj      qvalue                                            geneID #> 1 0.01357466 0.007144558 TNF/IL6/EGFR/AKT1/TP53/MYC/KRAS/PIK3CA/BRCA1/IL1B #> 2 0.64613328 0.340070147                       TNF/IL6/EGFR/AKT1/TP53/KRAS #> 3 0.06965583 0.036660965           TNF/EGFR/AKT1/TP53/MYC/KRAS/PIK3CA/IL1B #> 4 0.03506787 0.018456775      TNF/IL6/EGFR/AKT1/TP53/MYC/KRAS/PIK3CA/BRCA1 #>   Count foldEnrichment   ChemicalName #> 1    10       1.416667  Acetaminophen #> 2     6       1.020000        Arsenic #> 3     8       1.360000 Benzo(a)pyrene #> 4     9       1.390909      Cisplatin

previous_headings: Quick start > Step 3 – Run enrichment analysis
what: Build a synthetic expression matrix for CAMERA and GSVA
title: Introduction to ctdR
text: CAMERA GSVA take gene x sample expression matrix (typically log2 counts microarray intensities). vignette build small synthetic matrix using Entrez IDs already loaded import_CTD() :
code: # Pull the Entrez IDs covered by the bundled sample cache_dir <- rappdirs::user_cache_dir("ctdR") e <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) load(file.path(cache_dir, "ChemicalName_GeneEntrezIds.rda"),     envir = e) all_ids <- as.character(unique(unlist(     e$ChemicalName_GeneEntrezIds)))  # Synthetic expression matrix: 17 genes x 6 samples set.seed(2026) expr <- matrix(     rnorm(length(all_ids) * 6),     nrow = length(all_ids), ncol = 6,     dimnames = list(all_ids, paste0("S", 1:6)) ) dim(expr) #> [1] 17  6

previous_headings: Quick start > Step 3 – Run enrichment analysis
title: Introduction to ctdR
text: CAMERA tests, chemical, whether target genes show stronger differential signal rest transcriptome user-supplied design contrast, correcting inter-gene correlation within gene set. CAMERA output columns: ChemicalID, ChemicalName, NGenes (gene set size intersection rownames), Direction ("" / ""), pvalue, padj.
code: grp <- factor(rep(c("ctrl", "treat"), each = 3)) design <- model.matrix(~ grp)  camera_results <- enrichment_CTD(     expr,     method   = "CAMERA",     design   = design,     contrast = 2  # last column of design = treat vs ctrl ) head(camera_results) #>   ChemicalID     ChemicalName NGenes Direction     pvalue      padj #> 1    D002945        Cisplatin     11        Up 0.02319375 0.2319375 #> 2    D003907    Dexamethasone      7      Down 0.07151516 0.2383839 #> 3    D014635    Valproic Acid      8      Down 0.05895320 0.2383839 #> 4    D000082    Acetaminophen     12        Up 0.20432247 0.5108062 #> 5    D002104          Cadmium      8      Down 0.39170306 0.7189345 #> 6    D003520 Cyclophosphamide      5        Up 0.43136072 0.7189345

previous_headings: Quick start > Step 3 – Run enrichment analysis
title: Introduction to ctdR
text: GSVA produces per-sample enrichment score chemical, returning matrix (chemicals rows, samples columns) suitable clustering, association tests phenotypes, survival analysis, heatmap visualization. Tune underlying GSVA::gsvaParam() ..., example restrict gene sets given size:
code: gsva_scores <- enrichment_CTD(expr, method = "GSVA") #> ℹ GSVA version 2.6.1 #> ℹ Searching for rows with constant values #> ℹ Calculating GSVA ranks #> ℹ kcdf='auto' (default) #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Row-wise ECDF estimation with Gaussian kernels #> ℹ Calculating row ECDFs #> ℹ Calculating column ranks #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Calculating GSVA scores for 10 gene sets #> ✔ Calculations finished dim(gsva_scores)  # chemicals x samples #> [1] 10  6 head(gsva_scores, 3) #>                 S1        S2         S3        S4         S5          S6 #> D000082 -0.3073171 0.1181818 0.18181818 0.2000000  0.2770833  0.07017544 #> D001564 -0.3370536 0.4025974 0.01567944 0.3458647  0.2408638 -0.24068323 #> D002104  0.1014493 0.4313725 0.36585366 0.2833333 -0.0751634 -0.62903226 gsva_strict <- enrichment_CTD(     expr, method = "GSVA",     minSize = 5, maxSize = 500 ) #> ℹ GSVA version 2.6.1 #> ℹ Searching for rows with constant values #> ℹ Calculating GSVA ranks #> ℹ kcdf='auto' (default) #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Row-wise ECDF estimation with Gaussian kernels #> ℹ Calculating row ECDFs #> ℹ Calculating column ranks #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Calculating GSVA scores for 10 gene sets #> ✔ Calculations finished nrow(gsva_strict) #> [1] 10

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: plot_CTD() creates publication-ready plots enrichment results. auto-detects method: bar/dot plots fold enrichment ORA/GSEA, bar/dot plots -log10(padj) coloured direction CAMERA, sample-level heatmap top-variance chemicals GSVA.
code: # ORA bar plot of top enriched chemicals plot_CTD(ora_results, type = "bar") # ORA dot plot (size = gene count, color = adjusted p-value) plot_CTD(ora_results, type = "dot", n = 10) # CAMERA bar plot: x-axis = -log10(padj), fill = Direction plot_CTD(camera_results, type = "bar") # GSVA heatmap: top-variance chemicals across samples plot_CTD(gsva_scores)

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: default, enrichment_CTD() uses Benjamini-Hochberg ("BH") method p-value adjustment. can change via pAdjustMethod parameter: Available methods: "BH" (default), "bonferroni", "fdr" (alias BH), "none".
code: # Bonferroni correction (more conservative) ora_bonf <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA",     pAdjustMethod = "bonferroni") #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns  # No adjustment ora_raw <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA",     pAdjustMethod = "none") #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns  # Compare the adjusted p-value of the top hit data.frame(     method = c("BH (default)", "bonferroni", "none"),     top_padj = c(min(ora_results$padj),         min(ora_bonf$padj), min(ora_raw$padj)) ) #>         method    top_padj #> 1 BH (default) 0.013574661 #> 2   bonferroni 0.013574661 #> 3         none 0.003393665

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: Use ORA well-defined gene list significance threshold; GSEA leverage full ranking without cutoff; CAMERA proper multi-sample design want control inflated false-positive rate ORA/GSEA exhibit strongly co-regulated gene sets; GSVA want per-sample score feed downstream analyses (clustering, survival, correlation phenotypes).

title: Introduction to ctdR
text: CTD releases updated data periodically. update: Download latest CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz https://ctdbase.org/reports/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz. Decompress re-run import_CTD() – existing cache files overwritten.

what: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: ctdR R package identifies chemicals significantly associated set genes using data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD).

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: Four enrichment methods unified enrichment_CTD() interface, selected via method argument: ORA — -Representation Analysis (hypergeometric test). Input: gene list. Backend: clusterProfiler::enricher. GSEA — Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (rank-based, permutational). Input: ranked gene list. Backend: fgsea::fgsea. CAMERA — competitive gene-set test inter-gene correlation correction. Input: expression matrix + design + contrast. Backend: limma::camera. GSVA — Gene Set Variation Analysis (per-sample scoring). Input: expression matrix. Output: chemical × sample score matrix. Backend: GSVA::gsva. Additional features: Automatic caching — CTD data parsed cached locally fast repeated analyses Human-filtering — automatically restricts interactions Homo sapiens (OrganismID 9606) Auto-detected gene identifiers — Entrez vs HGNC SYMBOL detected rownames() input matrix (CAMERA / GSVA); override via id_type Visualization — plot_CTD() auto-dispatches bar/dot plot (ORA/GSEA/CAMERA) heatmap (GSVA)

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: package bundle, redistribute, embed data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database. CTD data created maintained NC State University subject specific licensing terms conditions. Users solely responsible : Downloading required data files directly https://ctdbase.org Reading accepting CTD Terms Service using data Complying applicable CTD data licensing requirements, including proper citation CTD publications derived works using ctdR CTD data, acknowledge read, understood, agreed CTD Terms Service.

previous_headings: Quick Start > Step 3 — Run enrichment analysis
title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
code: # Prepare your gene list as a data frame (Entrez IDs + numeric column) genes <- data.frame(   entrez_ids = c("7124", "3569", "7157", "672", "1956"),   pvalue     = c(0.001, 0.003, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05) )  # Over-Representation Analysis (default) ora_results <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA") head(ora_results)  # Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (uses the second column as ranking) gsea_results <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "GSEA") head(gsea_results)  # Customize multiple testing correction (default is "BH") ora_bonf <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA", pAdjustMethod = "bonferroni")

previous_headings: Quick Start > Step 3 — Run enrichment analysis
what: CAMERA — multi-sample paradigm with correlation correction
title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
code: # Suppose `expr` is a normalised expression matrix (genes x samples) # with Entrez IDs (or HGNC SYMBOLs) as rownames. grp    <- factor(c("ctrl","ctrl","ctrl","treat","treat","treat")) design <- model.matrix(~ grp)  camera_results <- enrichment_CTD(   expr,   method   = "CAMERA",   design   = design,   contrast = 2  # last column of `design` = treat vs ctrl ) head(camera_results)

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
code: # Returns a chemical x sample matrix of GSVA enrichment scores gsva_scores <- enrichment_CTD(expr, method = "GSVA") dim(gsva_scores)

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
code: # ORA / GSEA / CAMERA: bar or dot plot of top chemicals plot_CTD(ora_results,    type = "bar") plot_CTD(gsea_results,   type = "dot", n = 10) plot_CTD(camera_results, type = "bar")  # colour encodes Direction  # GSVA: heatmap of the top-variance chemicals across samples plot_CTD(gsva_scores)

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: genes × samples numeric matrix rownames(x) set either Entrez IDs HGNC SYMBOLs. Identifier type auto-detected rownames(x); override id_type argument needed.

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: numeric matrix CTD chemical IDs rows samples columns, containing per-sample enrichment scores (typically [-1, 1]). Unlike methods, GSVA return p-values — scores descriptive features downstream analyses (clustering, association outcomes, heatmap visualization).

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: ggplot2 — publication-quality plots readr — fast CSV reading rappdirs — cross-platform cache directory plyr — data manipulation

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: fgsea — fast GSEA implementation clusterProfiler — ORA enrichment limma — CAMERA backend (limma::camera()) GSVA — per-sample gene-set scoring AnnotationDbi — gene ID mapping org.Hs.eg.db — human gene annotation

title: ctdR — Chemical Enrichment Analysis using CTD
text: ctdR uses GitHub Actions continuous integration automated security auditing: security-scan workflow runs every push/PR weekly, includes four jobs: Dependency vulnerability audit — scans installed R packages Sonatype OSS Index via oysteR, flagging packages known CVEs. Static code analysis — runs lintr entire package source detect code quality potential security issues. Dependency review (PR ) — uses GitHub’s dependency-review-action flag new dependencies high-severity vulnerabilities merging. Secret & credential scan — uses TruffleHog detect accidentally committed secrets API keys repository history.

what: ctdR: Enrichment Analysis of Chemical-Gene Interactions from CTD — ctdR-package
title: ctdR: Enrichment Analysis of Chemical-Gene Interactions from CTD — ctdR-package
text: ctdR identifies chemicals significantly associated set genes using data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD, https://ctdbase.org). Four enrichment methods supported unified interface: ORA -Representation Analysis via     enricher. GSEA Gene Set Enrichment Analysis via     fgsea. CAMERA Competitive gene-set test accounting     inter-gene correlation, via camera. GSVA Per-sample Gene Set Variation Analysis via     gsva.

title: ctdR: Enrichment Analysis of Chemical-Gene Interactions from CTD — ctdR-package
text: Download CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz     https://ctdbase.org/reports/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz     decompress (gunzip CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz). Import data :     import_CTD("path//CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv") Run enrichment analysis:     enrichment_CTD(my_genes, method = "ORA")

title: ctdR: Enrichment Analysis of Chemical-Gene Interactions from CTD — ctdR-package
text: package bundle, redistribute, embed data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database. CTD data created maintained NC State University subject specific licensing terms conditions. Users solely responsible : Downloading required data files directly     https://ctdbase.org. Reading accepting CTD Terms Service     https://ctdbase.org//legal.jsp using data. Complying applicable CTD data licensing requirements,     including proper citation CTD publications derived works. using ctdR CTD data, acknowledge read, understood, agreed CTD Terms Service.

title: ctdR: Enrichment Analysis of Chemical-Gene Interactions from CTD — ctdR-package
text: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, Sciaky D, Wiegers J, Mattingly CJ (2023). "Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023." Nucleic Acids Research, 51(D1), D1257-D1262. doi:10.1093/nar/gkac833 . https://ctdbase.org

title: Competitive gene-set enrichment with CAMERA — .run_camera
text: Internal function runs CAMERA competitive gene-set test (camera) expression matrix cached CTD chemical gene sets. Unlike ORA/GSEA — take single (ranked) gene list — CAMERA tests, chemical, whether target genes show stronger differential signal rest transcriptome user-supplied design contrast, accounting inter-gene correlation.

title: Competitive gene-set enrichment with CAMERA — .run_camera
code: .run_camera(   expr,   design,   contrast,   id_type = NULL,   pAdjustMethod = "BH",   chemicals_meta,   cache_dir,   ... )

title: Competitive gene-set enrichment with CAMERA — .run_camera
text: expr Numeric expression matrix (genes x samples). rownames(expr) must Entrez IDs HGNC symbols matching cached CTD gene sets. design Design matrix produced e.g. model.matrix. contrast Either column number column name design, numeric contrast vector. id_type Either "entrez", "symbol", NULL auto-detection rownames(expr). pAdjustMethod Method passed p.adjust compute padj column. chemicals_meta Data frame columns ChemicalID ChemicalName, used annotate results. cache_dir Directory holding cached CTD .rda files. ... Forwarded camera (e.g. inter.gene.cor, use.ranks, allow.neg.cor, trend.var).

title: Competitive gene-set enrichment with CAMERA — .run_camera
text: data frame columns ChemicalID, ChemicalName,   NGenes, Direction, Correlation (reported),   pvalue, padj, sorted padj ascending.

title: Per-sample gene-set scoring with GSVA — .run_gsva
text: Internal function runs Gene Set Variation Analysis (gsva) expression matrix using cached CTD chemical gene sets. Unlike ORA/GSEA/CAMERA — return single p-value per chemical (group-level inference) — GSVA produces matrix per-sample enrichment scores: one row per chemical, one column per sample. scores suitable downstream clustering, association tests phenotypes, survival analysis, heatmap visualization.

title: Per-sample gene-set scoring with GSVA — .run_gsva
Per-sample gene-set scoring with GSVA — .run_gsva
text: expr Numeric expression matrix (genes x samples). rownames(expr) must Entrez IDs HGNC symbols matching cached CTD gene sets. id_type Either "entrez", "symbol", NULL auto-detection rownames(expr). cache_dir Directory holding cached CTD .rda files. ... Forwarded gsvaParam (e.g. kcdf, minSize, maxSize, tau, maxDiff).

title: Per-sample gene-set scoring with GSVA — .run_gsva
text: numeric matrix GSVA enrichment scores CTD chemical IDs   rows samples columns.

what: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
text: Identifies chemicals whose known gene targets significantly enriched user-supplied gene list expression matrix, using data Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD). Four methods available: ORA -Representation Analysis (default). Tests whether     overlap gene list chemical's target genes     larger expected chance.     Uses enricher.     Input: data frame Entrez IDs (column entrez_ids)     optional numeric value column. GSEA Gene Set Enrichment Analysis. Uses ranked gene list     (ranked numeric column input, e.g. p-values fold     changes) detect chemicals whose targets cluster toward top     bottom ranking. Uses fgsea. CAMERA Competitive gene-set test accounting     inter-gene correlation. Uses camera.     Input: numeric expression matrix (genes x samples) plus design     matrix contrast. GSVA Gene Set Variation Analysis (sample-level scoring).     Returns matrix per-sample enrichment scores chemical,     suitable downstream clustering association testing.     Uses gsva.     Input: numeric expression matrix (genes x samples).

title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
code: enrichment_CTD(   x,   method = c("ORA", "GSEA", "CAMERA", "GSVA"),   design = NULL,   contrast = NULL,   id_type = NULL,   pAdjustMethod = "BH",   ... )

title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
text: x input. expected type depends method: "ORA" "GSEA": data frame least two     columns, entrez_ids (character numeric Entrez gene IDs)     numeric value column (e.g. p-value, log fold-change).     numeric column used ranking GSEA ignored ORA. "CAMERA" "GSVA": numeric expression     matrix genes rows samples columns. rownames(x)     must either Entrez IDs HGNC SYMBOLs. method Character. Enrichment method: "ORA" (default), "GSEA", "CAMERA", "GSVA". design Design matrix (required method = "CAMERA"). contrast Contrast specification camera (column number, column name, numeric vector). Required method = "CAMERA". id_type Either "entrez", "symbol", NULL (default) auto-detection rownames(x). used method "CAMERA" "GSVA". pAdjustMethod Character. Method multiple testing correction: one "BH" (Benjamini-Hochberg, default), "bonferroni", "fdr" (alias BH), "none". used method = "GSVA" (returns scores rather p-values). ... Additional arguments forwarded underlying engine: enricher ORA, fgsea GSEA, camera CAMERA, gsva GSVA.

title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
text: "ORA", "GSEA", "CAMERA": data frame     enrichment results sorted adjusted p-value (padj). "GSVA": numeric matrix enrichment scores     chemicals (CTD chemical IDs) rows samples columns.

title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD

title: Chemical Enrichment Analysis Using CTD — enrichment_CTD
code: # Import the bundled sample data first: sample_file <- system.file(     "extdata", "CTD_chem_gene_ixns_sample.csv",     package = "ctdR" ) import_CTD(sample_file) #> Reading CTD chemical-gene interactions from: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.6/Resources/library/ctdR/extdata/CTD_chem_gene_ixns_sample.csv #> Filtered to 86 human interactions #> Mapping genes for 10 chemicals (this may take a while)... #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> CTD data cached successfully in: ~/Library/Caches/ctdR  # ORA / GSEA: prepare a gene list with Entrez IDs and a numeric value genes <- data.frame(     entrez_ids = c("7124", "3569", "7157", "672", "1956"),     pvalue = c(0.001, 0.003, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05) ) ora_results <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "ORA") #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns gsea_results <- enrichment_CTD(genes, method = "GSEA") #> Warning: All values in the stats vector are greater than zero and scoreType is "std", maybe you should switch to scoreType = "pos".  # CAMERA: expression matrix + design + contrast set.seed(1) expr <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 6), nrow = 20,     dimnames = list(c("7124","3569","7157","672","1956",         "1017","1019","207","208","595",         "894","983","1029","1869","5599",         "5290","4609","6347","3458","2099"),         paste0("S", 1:6))) grp <- factor(rep(c("ctrl","treat"), each = 3)) d <- model.matrix(~ grp) # \donttest{ camera_results <- enrichment_CTD(expr, method = "CAMERA",     design = d, contrast = 2)  # GSVA: per-sample enrichment scores gsva_scores <- enrichment_CTD(expr, method = "GSVA") #> ℹ GSVA version 2.6.1 #> ℹ Searching for rows with constant values #> ℹ Calculating GSVA ranks #> ℹ kcdf='auto' (default) #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Row-wise ECDF estimation with Gaussian kernels #> ℹ Calculating row ECDFs #> ℹ Calculating column ranks #> ℹ GSVA dense (classical) algorithm #> ℹ Calculating GSVA scores for 10 gene sets #> ✔ Calculations finished # }

title: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — gsea
text: Internal function performs Gene Set Enrichment Analysis using fgsea. Detects chemicals whose known target genes cluster toward extremes ranked gene list.

title: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — gsea
Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — gsea
text: ChemicalName_GeneEntrezIds named list element character vector Entrez gene IDs associated CTD chemical. Names CTD chemical IDs. entrez_ids data frame least two columns: entrez_ids Entrez gene IDs (character). (second column) Numeric values used ranking (e.g. p-values).     Converted internally -log10(value)     ranking statistic. chemicals data frame columns ChemicalID ChemicalName, used annotate results human-readable names. pAdjustMethod Character. Method multiple testing correction (default "BH"). Passed p.adjust recalculate padj column returned fgsea.

title: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — gsea
text: data frame columns: ChemicalID, pval,   padj, ES, NES, size, leadingEdge,   ChemicalName, foldEnrichment, Enriched_GENE.   Results sorted padj ascending order.

what: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
title: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
text: Parses, filters, caches CTD chemical-gene interactions file can used enrichment_CTD. function must called running enrichment analysis. raw data file must downloaded manually CTD website. required file CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz, available https://ctdbase.org/reports/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz. Decompress (e.g. gunzip CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv.gz) pass resulting CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv file path function.

title: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
text: file_path Character. Path decompressed CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv file (e.g. "~/Downloads/CTD_chem_gene_ixns.csv").

title: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect caching   processed data.

title: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
text: Processing steps performed import_CTD: Reads CSV, skipping 27 CTD header lines. Filters interactions Homo sapiens (OrganismID 9606). chemical, collects associated Entrez gene IDs. Maps Entrez IDs HGNC gene symbols via org.Hs.eg.db. Saves three cached objects (chemicals,     ChemicalName_GeneEntrezIds,     ChemicalName_GeneSymbols) user cache     directory (user_cache_dir). cache stored rappdirs::user_cache_dir("ctdR"). re-import (e.g. downloading newer CTD release), simply call import_CTD() — existing cache files overwritten.

title: Import CTD Chemical-Gene Interaction Data — import_CTD
text: package bundle redistribute CTD data. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database maintained NC State University data subject specific licensing terms. Users responsible downloading data directly https://ctdbase.org complying CTD Terms Service (https://ctdbase.org//legal.jsp). using function acknowledge read accepted terms.

Method multiple testing correction (default "BH"). Passed enricher.

title: Plot Chemical Enrichment Results — plot_CTD
text: Visualizes output enrichment_CTD. function auto-detects enrichment method input class column structure: ORA / GSEA (data frame Count     NES): bar dot plot fold enrichment. CAMERA (data frame Direction NGenes):     bar dot plot \(-\log_{10}(\mathrm{padj})\), colored     direction enrichment. GSVA (numeric matrix chemicals x samples): heatmap     per-sample enrichment scores top-N chemicals selected     score variance across samples.

title: Plot Chemical Enrichment Results — plot_CTD
Plot Chemical Enrichment Results — plot_CTD
text: results data frame numeric matrix returned enrichment_CTD. type Character. Plot type tabular results: "bar" (default) "dot". Ignored GSVA matrix input. n Integer. Number top chemicals display (default 20). Selection criterion: ascending padj tabular results, descending score variance across samples GSVA matrices. title Character. Plot title. NULL (default), title generated automatically based detected method.

title: Plot Chemical Enrichment Results — plot_CTD
text: ggplot2 object.

what: Internal input helpers for ctdR — utils-input
title: Internal input helpers for ctdR — utils-input
text: Shared helpers used matrix-based enrichment methods (CAMERA, GSVA): identifier type detection rownames gene-set loading CTD cache.

title: Changes in version 0.99.2
text: "ORA" (default) — -Representation Analysis via clusterProfiler::enricher() (unchanged). "GSEA" — rank-based Gene Set Enrichment Analysis via fgsea::fgsea() (unchanged). "CAMERA" — competitive gene-set test accounting inter-gene correlation, via limma::camera(). Input: numeric expression matrix (genes x samples) plus design matrix contrast. "GSVA" — per-sample Gene Set Variation Analysis via GSVA::gsva(), returning chemical x sample score matrix. New first argument x (polymorphic): data frame ORA/GSEA, numeric matrix CAMERA/GSVA. Auto-detects identifier type (Entrez vs HGNC SYMBOL) rownames(x); explicit id_type override also accepted. plot_CTD() now dispatches input class method-specific columns: bar/dot plots fold enrichment ORA/GSEA, bar/dot plots -log10(padj) coloured direction enrichment CAMERA, sample-level heatmap top-variance chemicals GSVA.

title: Changes in version 0.99.2
text: first argument enrichment_CTD() renamed entrez_ids -> x. Calls using old name still work emit deprecation warning removed future release.

title: Changes in version 0.99.2
text: New Imports: limma, GSVA, stats.

title: Changes in version 0.99.1
text: Removed renv package. Dependencies managed via DESCRIPTION installed r-lib/actions/setup-r-dependencies (pak-based) CI.

title: Changes in version 0.99.1
text: import_CTD() now stores ChemicalName_GeneSymbols$gene character vector instead factor, fixing “universe must character vector” clusterProfiler::enricher(). Inlined parse_ratio() private helper DOSE::parse_ratio longer exported. DOSE dropped Imports. Aligned man/gsea.Rd parameter name R/gsea.R (ChemicalName_GeneEntrezIds), removing codoc-mismatch WARNING. Declared plot_CTD() ggplot2 NSE column references utils::globalVariables(), removing “visible binding” NOTEs.

title: Changes in version 0.99.1
text: Pinned trufflesecurity/trufflehog concrete version; bare @v3 tag exist action repo. Made oysteR audit fail-soft OSS Index credentials missing (still fails build real vulnerabilities). Removed dependency-review job: GitHub’s Dependency Graph support R DESCRIPTION files.

title: Changes in version 0.99.0
text: Bumped version 0.99.0 Bioconductor submission. Fixed R CMD check pass 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes. Fixed CI workflows macOS, Ubuntu, Windows. Added Codecov integration coverage reporting. Added GitHub Pages site usage examples. Fixed broken README badges.

title: Changes in version 0.1.2
text: Added import_CTD() function import cache CTD chemical-gene interaction data user-downloaded files. Added -Representation Analysis (ORA) method via clusterProfiler::enricher. enrichment_CTD() now supports two methods: "ORA" (default) "GSEA". Clear error message CTD data imported, instructions download import required file.

title: Changes in version 0.1.2
text: Separated data import analysis — users call import_CTD() , enrichment_CTD() many times needed. Added fold enrichment calculation ORA GSEA results. gsea() now receives chemicals explicit parameter instead relying parent environment. Removed deprecated nperm parameter fgsea::fgsea call. Removed leftover browser() calls.

title: Changes in version 0.1.2
text: Added comprehensive roxygen documentation exported internal functions, including parameter descriptions, return value tables, examples. Added package-level help page (?ctdR) quick start guide. Added data licensing disclaimer throughout documentation DESCRIPTION. Added README.md badges, installation instructions, usage examples. Added vignette complete workflow.

title: Changes in version 0.1.2
text: Added testthat test suite 100% line coverage. Tests cover: import validation, missing data errors, ORA enrichment, GSEA enrichment, NA handling, column structure.

title: Changes in version 0.1.2
text: Added GitHub Actions CI macOS, Ubuntu, Windows. Added test coverage workflow Codecov integration.

title: Changes in version 0.1.1
text: Added GSEA analysis via fgsea::fgsea. Initial caching mechanism using rappdirs.

title: Changes in version 0.1.0
text: Initial package skeleton. Basic gene-chemical interaction parsing CTD CSV files.
